entry.value:
[["currentConversationId","pins","agentExecutionMode","isPanelCollapsed","displayedAnnouncements","conversations","onboarding","a|0|1|2|3|4|5|6","322d26f8-8c26-447b-bab6-9193f87dea9c","o|","auto","b|T","a|","1ee2e0fb-effe-4361-8587-e6e60ee64ecd","beac2010-20a3-4759-85b6-01d10ea2ea0a","a|D|E|8","id","createdAtIso","lastInteractedAtIso","feedbackStates","selectedModelId","requestIds","isPinned","isShareable","extraData","personaType","toolUseStates","chatHistory","draftExchange","a|G|H|I|J|K|L|M|N|O|P|Q|R|S","2025-11-21T07:26:04.692Z","claude-sonnet-4-5","b|F","isAgentConversation","hasDirtyEdits","a|X|Y","o|Z|B|W","n|0","request_message","rich_text_json_repr","mentioned_items","status","a|c|d|e|f","","type","content","a|i|j","doc","a|i","paragraph","o|m|n","a|o","o|k|l|p","draft","o|g|h|q|C|r","o|T|D|U|U|9|V|C|W|W|a|b|9|C|s","name","rootTaskUuid","a|G|u|H|I|J|K|L|M|N|O|P|v|Q|R|S","Multi-Channel Intelligence Desk Architecture
","2025-11-21T07:26:04.757Z","2026-03-14T01:57:47.280Z","claude-sonnet-4-6","hasTitleGenerated","a|X|Y|11","o|12|B|B|B","1ca4ff4a-5f02-4a59-9b1a-b562cfd7e172","a|c|d|f","o|15|h|q|r","o|w|E|x|y|z|9|10|C|W|B|13|b|14|9|C|16","a|G|u|H|I|K|L|M|N|O|v|J|Q|R|S","Phase 4 Integration and Refinement
","2026-03-14T01:58:00.434Z","2026-03-19T21:11:22.488Z","baselineTimestamp","a|X|Y|11|1C","o|1D|B|B|B|b","715a395b-0dab-4f1e-8495-a1561a857faa","a|c|d|f|e","The current training run has the following observable issues that need to be diagnosed and fixed:

1. **High calibration error**: The best validation ECE after 5 epochs is ~0.372, and per-SignalType mean absolute error is ~0.52 across all 18 classes. For a well-calibrated model on a real dataset, ECE should be below 0.05. Investigate and fix the root cause — specifically, determine whether the synthetic dataset's `confidence` values are meaningfully correlated with the `is_hard_negative` label, and if not, redesign `training/generate_dataset.py` so that hard negatives have systematically higher confidence values and regular examples have confidence values that are genuinely predictive of the true label. The calibrator should be able to learn a meaningful temperature correction from the data.

2. **Early stopping behaviour**: In the previous run, early stopping did not trigger because ECE improved at every epoch (including after the LR decay at epoch 3). Verify that the early stopping logic in `training/train.py` is correct — specifically that `EARLY_STOP_MIN_DELTA = 0.001` and `EARLY_STOP_PATIENCE = 2` are appropriate thresholds for the dataset size (8,010 training examples), and adjust them if needed so early stopping fires when the model has genuinely converged rather than only when ECE is oscillating.

3. **Checkpoint pruning**: After the last run, 3 checkpoint files exist (`epoch_002`, `epoch_004`, `epoch_005`). Confirm that `MAX_CHECKPOINTS = 3` is being enforced correctly and that the pruning logic deletes the oldest files (by epoch number, not by filesystem mtime) when a 4th checkpoint would be written.

4. **Wall-clock time dominated by disk I/O**: The training run took 7.81 s wall-clock for 40,050 updates, with 4.13 s in system time — indicating that `ConfidenceCalibrator._save()` (which writes `calibration_state.json` after every single update) is consuming more than half the total runtime. Implement a batched save strategy in `training/train.py`: accumulate gradient updates in memory and call `_save()` only once per epoch (after all updates for that epoch are complete), rather than after every individual update. Do not modify `ConfidenceCalibrator._save()` itself; instead, patch or defer the save at the training loop level.

Do not modify any file outside `training/` and `app/intelligence/calibration.py`. After all fixes, run `python training/generate_dataset.py && python training/train.py` and confirm:
- Final best validation ECE is below **0.10**.
- Wall-clock time for the training run is under **3 seconds**.
- `python -m pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/llm/test_load.py -q` still reports **587 passed, 20 skipped, 0 errors**.","text","a|i|1I","The current training run has the following observable issues that need to be diagnosed and fixed:","o|1J|1I|1K","hardBreak","o|m|1M","1. **High calibration error**: The best validation ECE after 5 epochs is ~0.372, and per-SignalType mean absolute error is ~0.52 across all 18 classes. For a well-calibrated model on a real dataset, ECE should be below 0.05. Investigate and fix the root cause — specifically, determine whether the synthetic dataset's `confidence` values are meaningfully correlated with the `is_hard_negative` label, and if not, redesign `training/generate_dataset.py` so that hard negatives have systematically higher confidence values and regular examples have confidence values that are genuinely predictive of the true label. The calibrator should be able to learn a meaningful temperature correction from the data.","o|1J|1I|1O","2. **Early stopping behaviour**: In the previous run, early stopping did not trigger because ECE improved at every epoch (including after the LR decay at epoch 3). Verify that the early stopping logic in `training/train.py` is correct — specifically that `EARLY_STOP_MIN_DELTA = 0.001` and `EARLY_STOP_PATIENCE = 2` are appropriate thresholds for the dataset size (8,010 training examples), and adjust them if needed so early stopping fires when the model has genuinely converged rather than only when ECE is oscillating.","o|1J|1I|1Q","3. **Checkpoint pruning**: After the last run, 3 checkpoint files exist (`epoch_002`, `epoch_004`, `epoch_005`). Confirm that `MAX_CHECKPOINTS = 3` is being enforced correctly and that the pruning logic deletes the oldest files (by epoch number, not by filesystem mtime) when a 4th checkpoint would be written.","o|1J|1I|1S","4. **Wall-clock time dominated by disk I/O**: The training run took 7.81 s wall-clock for 40,050 updates, with 4.13 s in system time — indicating that `ConfidenceCalibrator._save()` (which writes `calibration_state.json` after every single update) is consuming more than half the total runtime. Implement a batched save strategy in `training/train.py`: accumulate gradient updates in memory and call `_save()` only once per epoch (after all updates for that epoch are complete), rather than after every individual update. Do not modify `ConfidenceCalibrator._save()` itself; instead, patch or defer the save at the training loop level.","o|1J|1I|1U","Do not modify any file outside `training/` and `app/intelligence/calibration.py`. After all fixes, run `python training/generate_dataset.py && python training/train.py` and confirm:","o|1J|1I|1W","- Final best validation ECE is below **0.10**.","o|1J|1I|1Y","- Wall-clock time for the training run is under **3 seconds**.","o|1J|1I|1a","- `python -m pytest tests/ --ignore=tests/llm/test_load.py -q` still reports **587 passed, 20 skipped, 0 errors**.","o|1J|1I|1c","a|1L|1N|1N|1P|1N|1N|1R|1N|1N|1T|1N|1N|1V|1N|1N|1X|1N|1Z|1N|1b|1N|1d","o|k|n|1e","a|1f","o|k|l|1g","o|1G|1H|1h|r|C","o|18|8|19|1A|1B|10|C|W|W|1E|1F|9|9|C|1i","o|F|t|17|1j","shown","workspaceQuestions","a|1l|1m","o|1n|W|C","o|7|8|9|A|B|C|1k|1o"],"1p"]
